mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above 110 signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5 signals a labor market tightening that reduces the labor supply and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5 signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in monetary tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 105 signals a supply-driven contraction that reduces corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5 signals a labor market loosening that reduces the labor supply and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market loosening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below 1.0 million signals a demand-driven contraction that reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
